a: Article JSON-LD must include: @context https://schema.org, @type Article, headline (50-60 chars ideal), description (120-160 chars meta-style), image (URL or ImageObject), author (Person), publisher (Organization with logo), datePublished, dateModified, inLanguage (BCP-47 like en, hi, es), and url. Use isAccessibleForFree when content is free. For worldwide SEO, write headline in the page language and set inLanguage correctly.

q: Product schema.org JSON-LD required fields SEO
a: Product schema requires: name, description, image, brand (@type Brand), offers (@type Offer with price, priceCurrency ISO 4217 like USD/INR/EUR, availability schema.org/InStock), sku or gtin when available. Add aggregateRating for star snippets. Use review array for rich results. priceValidUntil boosts ecommerce trust. Support worldwide currencies: USD, EUR, GBP, INR, JPY, AED, BRL.

FAQPage uses mainEntity array of Question objects. Each Question needs name (the question text) and acceptedAnswer (@type Answer with text). Write questions in the user's language (Hindi, English, etc.). Google shows FAQ rich results when content matches visible page FAQs. Keep answers concise (2-4 sentences). inLanguage should match page locale.

LocalBusiness needs: name, description, image, url, telephone, address (PostalAddress with streetAddress, addressLocality, addressRegion, postalCode, addressCountry ISO code), openingHours (Mo-Fr 09:00-18:00 format), geo (GeoCoordinates latitude/longitude). Add priceRange ($ to $$$$) for restaurants/shops. Works worldwide — use correct country codes: IN, US, GB, AE, DE, FR, JP.

JobPosting requires: title, description, datePosted (ISO 8601), hiringOrganization (@type Organization), jobLocation (Place with PostalAddress), employmentType (FULL_TIME, PART_TIME, CONTRACTOR, INTERN). Add baseSalary with currency for better listings. validThrough for expiry. Works for remote jobs: jobLocationType TELECOMMUTE.

q: Recipe schema markup JSON-LD best practices
a: Recipe schema: name, description, image, author, datePublished, recipeIngredient (array of strings), recipeInstructions (HowToStep or text), prepTime/cookTime/totalTime in ISO 8601 duration (PT30M). Add recipeYield, recipeCategory, recipeCuisine for worldwide cuisines (Indian, Italian, Mexican, Japanese). nutrition (NutritionInformation) optional.

Event schema: name, startDate, endDate (ISO 8601 with timezone), location (Place or VirtualLocation for online), description, image, eventStatus (EventScheduled), eventAttendanceMode (OfflineEventAttendanceMode, OnlineEventAttendanceMode, MixedEventAttendanceMode). Add organizer and offers for ticketed events worldwide.

Organization schema: name, url, logo (ImageObject or URL), description, sameAs (social profile URLs array), contactPoint (@type ContactPoint with telephone, contactType customer service, areaServed worldwide). For Spanish sites set inLanguage es. Use foundingDate, numberOfEmployees for enterprise profiles.

Course schema: name, description, provider (Organization), offers (price, currency), hasCourseInstance (CourseInstance with courseMode online/onsite/blended). Add educationalLevel, inLanguage, teaches skills. Ideal for MOOCs, bootcamps, and training platforms worldwide.

q: HowTo schema markup JSON-LD best practices
a: HowTo schema: name, description, image, totalTime, step array of HowToStep each with name and text (or itemListElement). Add tool and supply arrays when relevant. Google shows step-by-step rich results. Write steps in the page language; set inLanguage.

SoftwareApplication: name, description, applicationCategory (BusinessApplication, GameApplication, etc.), operatingSystem (Windows, macOS, Android, iOS, Web), offers (price 0 for free apps), screenshot, softwareVersion, author (Organization). Mobile apps can use MobileApplication @type instead.

MobileApplication extends SoftwareApplication. Include operatingSystem (Android 8+, iOS 15+), applicationCategory, offers, aggregateRating, screenshot array, downloadUrl or installUrl (Play Store / App Store links). inLanguage for localized app store pages.

Hotel (LodgingBusiness): name, description, image, url, telephone, address, starRating (Rating), amenityFeature (WiFi, Pool, Parking), priceRange. geo coordinates help maps. checkinTime/checkoutTime optional. Works for resorts, hostels, boutique hotels worldwide.

Restaurant schema: name, image, address, telephone, servesCuisine (Indian, Chinese, Italian, etc.), priceRange, openingHoursSpecification, menu url, acceptsReservations. aggregateRating for star display. Geo for local SEO.

Book schema: name, author (Person), isbn, publisher, datePublished, bookFormat (EBook, Hardcover, Paperback), inLanguage, numberOfPages, image (cover), offers. Ideal for publishers, authors, and bookstores globally.

Movie schema: name, description, image, director (Person), actor array, datePublished, duration (ISO 8601 PT2H), genre, aggregateRating, productionCompany. trailer (VideoObject) optional for rich results.

VideoObject: name, description, thumbnailUrl, uploadDate, duration (PT format), contentUrl or embedUrl, publisher. Add inLanguage and caption for accessibility. Required for video rich results on Google worldwide.

Person schema: name, jobTitle, image, url, sameAs (LinkedIn, Twitter, GitHub), worksFor (Organization), alumniOf, knowsAbout skills array. Ideal for personal portfolios, authors, and executives. No fake credentials — use only provided data.

BreadcrumbList: itemListElement array of ListItem with position (1-based), name, and item (URL). Last item is current page. Helps Google show breadcrumb trail in SERPs worldwide. Keep names short and translated to page language.

Review schema: itemReviewed (Product or Organization), reviewRating (@type Rating with ratingValue, bestRating), author (Person), reviewBody text, datePublished. Pair with Product schema via @id linking for ecommerce.

MedicalBusiness and Dentist: name, description, medicalSpecialty, address, telephone, openingHours, image, priceRange. areaServed for multi-city clinics. healthPlanNetwork optional. Use correct schema type Dentist for dental practices.

EducationalOrganization: name, url, logo, address, telephone, sameAs, description. alumni array optional. department for faculties. Works for schools, universities, and training institutes in any country.
GovernmentOrganization: name, url, logo, address, contactPoint, description. areaServed country or region. Use for public sector portals, ministries, and municipal websites worldwide.

FinancialService and LegalService extend LocalBusiness/Service. Include name, description, address, telephone, areaServed, priceRange, openingHours. serviceType describes offerings (loans, insurance, litigation, contracts). Regulated industries: avoid unverifiable claims.

TouristAttraction: name, description, image, address, geo, openingHours, isAccessibleForFree boolean, touristType. Ideal for museums, monuments, parks, landmarks worldwide. Add aggregateRating when reviews exist.

Store schema: name, description, url, image, address, telephone, openingHours, priceRange, paymentAccepted, currenciesAccepted (USD, INR, EUR). ParentOrganization links to brand HQ. For online-only stores use OnlineStore implied via url.

q: schema markup Article multilingual en hi es fr ar
a: Multilingual schema rules: always set inLanguage to BCP-47 code (en, hi, es, fr, ar, de, ja, zh). Write name, headline, description in the same language as the page. Use unicode in JSON-LD (ensure_ascii false). hreflang tags on HTML complement JSON-LD but are separate. For Hindi content use Devanagari in text fields.

q: schema markup JSON-LD worldwide SEO advanced
a: Advanced worldwide SEO with JSON-LD: 1) One primary @type per page. 2) Never invent fake reviews or ratings. 3) Use ISO dates and currencies. 4) Image URLs must be absolute HTTPS. 5) Combine WebSite + WebPage on homepage. 6) Product + Offer for ecommerce. 7) Validate with Google Rich Results Test. 8) Keep JSON-LD under 100KB. 9) Match visible page content exactly.

NewsArticle: time-sensitive journalism with datePublished critical. Blog: blog section or index. WebPage: generic page fallback. Article: evergreen guides. All share headline, author, publisher, image. Pick the most specific @type that matches content.

AggregateRating: ratingValue (e.g. 4.7), reviewCount, bestRating (usually 5), worstRating (usually 1). Must reflect real reviews on page. Attach to Product, LocalBusiness, Recipe, or Organization. Google may show star snippets.

PodcastEpisode: name, datePublished, partOfSeries (PodcastSeries), description, audio contentUrl, duration. MusicRecording: name, byArtist (Person/MusicGroup), duration, inAlbum optional. Ideal for media platforms and artists worldwide.

For international job boards set inLanguage on description, use jobLocation with correct addressCountry, employmentType per local norms. baseSalary with currency and unitText (YEAR, MONTH, HOUR). Remote: jobLocationType TELECOMMUTE.
RealEstateAgent: name, address, telephone, url, image, areaServed (city/region), priceRange. memberOf for brokerage affiliation. Works for agencies in US, UK, India, UAE, and globally.

WebSite with potentialAction SearchAction enables sitelinks search box in Google. target URL must include {search_term_string} placeholder. query-input required name=search_term_string. url is site homepage.
Offer standalone: price, priceCurrency, availability, url, validFrom/validThrough. Brand: name, logo, description, sameAs social links. Link Brand inside Product.brand for manufacturer clarity.

ImageObject: contentUrl (direct image link), name, description, caption, width, height, license, creator. Use for photography portfolios, stock pages, and media galleries. inLanguage for caption translation.
